channel.title: Errors on WordPress FAQ
channel.description: Recent content in Errors on WordPress FAQ

item.title: How to Fix a Hacked WordPress Site
item.description: The community consensus is that most hacks stem from outdated plugins or poor hosting security, requiring a clean reinstall to fully recover. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 8. Known Issues Backdoor files hiding in hosting accounts Malicious code in plugins Insecure PHP versions on shared hosting Database injection Community Q&A How do I clean a hacked WordPress site? Start with a fresh WordPress installation, import your database SQL file, and manually migrate only your /wp-content/uploads folder.

item.title: Troubleshooting WooCommerce Checkout Issues
item.description: Checkout failures are typically caused by theme block conflicts, plugin incompatibilities, or improper caching of the checkout page flow. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 9. Known Issues Checkout page block vs shortcode conflict Caching of checkout and cart pages Plugin conflicts causing script failures Community Q&A Why is my WooCommerce checkout page not working? Common causes include theme block conflicts, caching plugins interfering with dynamic checkout scripts, or plugin conflicts.

item.title: WordPress File Permissions and Security Best Practices
item.description: Standard WordPress file permissions should be set to 755 for directories and 644 for files to ensure optimal security and functionality. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 9. Known Issues Permission denied errors on managed hosting Malware persistence due to incorrect file ownership SFTP access limitations on managed platforms Community Q&A What are the recommended file permissions for WordPress? Directories should generally be set to 755 and individual files to 644 to balance security and server accessibility.
